areas_completed: Item type filters and evaluation labels

Peek interaction shape.question: How should the quick peek open?
Peek interaction shape.answer: Hover/focus preview + click opens item page
options_presented: Hover/focus preview + click opens item page
Click/tap opens a pinned peek card
Only use the existing item page

Peek interaction shape.question: When the peek appears, how should it behave?
How should users dismiss or move through peeks?
What should click/tap do from a peek?
options_presented: Pin the peek in place first

Peek card content and fallback.question: What should the peek show first?
When there is no screenshot or preview image, what should the peek show?
What metadata belongs inside the peek without making it crowded?
Peek card content and fallback.answer: Almost entirely visual; at most a tiny title/name above it; avoid metadata unless there is no visual; do not recreate item card inside peek

Peek card content and fallback.question: When the peek is only a placeholder because no visual exists yet, how much explanation should it include?
Peek card content and fallback.answer: Minimal placeholder: Preview not available yet + Open component page

Item type filters and evaluation labels.question: Where should item type filters live?
Item type filters and evaluation labels.answer: Compact filter bar above results with clear reset; evolved into an extensible filter picker instead of fixed chips

Item type filters and evaluation labels.question: Which item types should be visible as filter options?
Item type filters and evaluation labels.answer: Do not hardcode a tiny list. Pull dynamically from loaded itemSummaries in public/data/registries.json via loadRegistries.ts. Expose all useful available values from current metadata, including type, category, tags, visual availability, and catalog/status when present.
options_presented: Only types present in current results

Item type filters and evaluation labels.question: How should filters behave as the vocabulary grows?
Item type filters and evaluation labels.answer: Use command-menu style + Filter picker. Selecting a value adds a removable badge/tag. Same filter system should work consistently across discovery, profile, and relevant route/sidebar/component surfaces.

Item type filters and evaluation labels.question: How should evaluation/status labels behave?
Item type filters and evaluation labels.answer: Remove or demote distracting labels such as verified item and high confidence. Main point is quick component viewing, not confidence/status noise. Keep only useful details and a couple of badges where they help; avoid blowing up cards or sidebars. Long lists and pill groups should become scrollable once long enough.
options_presented: Keep labels compact and mostly off cards
Show evaluation badges on every card
Only show evaluation labels on item detail page

Item type filters and evaluation labels.question: What existing route/sidebar areas need cleanup?
Item type filters and evaluation labels.answer: Existing route views and sidebars are too broad and inconsistent. Expand groupings beyond a handful of broad buckets; make selectors and pill lists more complete, scrollable, and consistent. Registries-by-component badges need a better overflow/disclosure design or should be removed from cramped rows. Docs and registry homepage links are fine; profile/view component/details can remain if they do not distract.
options_presented: Freeform feedback from user with selectors .app-inner > main > aside, aside > #aside > .pill-list, .app-inner > main > .content

Related alternatives behavior.question: How should similar components show up?
Related alternatives behavior.answer: Small Related/Similar components strip on item/peek detail surfaces, based on shared type/category/tags and clearly not presented as quality-ranked yet.
options_presented: Small Related components strip on item/peek detail surfaces
Inline alternatives inside every discovery card

Related alternatives behavior.question: What should the strip contain?
Related alternatives behavior.answer: Alternative navigator rather than metadata cards: click a component, see related alternatives, click an alternative and continue browsing to the next related component. Minimal visual/placeholder plus name/registry is enough.

Related alternatives behavior.question: What is the long-term alternatives product direction?
Related alternatives behavior.answer: Production pattern upgrades: help users move from generic AI-generated UI toward more complete, polished, production-style component patterns. Phase 8 prepares the browsing/data seam and must not claim upgrade/better/correct unless evidence exists.

Related alternatives behavior.question: What should Phase 8 call this in the visible UI?
Related alternatives behavior.answer: Use safe visible labels such as Similar patterns or Related components for now. Preserve the future concept as production pattern upgrades / upgrade paths in planning so a later evidence-backed recommendation milestone can promote the stronger language.
options_presented: Upgrade paths / production pattern upgrades reserved for future evidence-backed wording

deferred_ideas: Interactive matrix redesign with complete dynamic component/tag coverage belongs to v1.3 Dynamic Coverage Matrix unless Phase 8 planner can make a tiny preparatory consistency cleanup without expanding scope.
Evidence-backed production pattern upgrades / upgrade paths for replacing generic AI-generated UI belong to a future recommendation milestone after Registry Atlas has enough examples and signals.
